instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-under-80d.html
Term Insurance Tax Benefits Under 80D
Term insurance is a pure protection plan that is valid for a term or specific period on payment of a defined premium. In the unfortunate event of the insured person’s death during the term, the nominee receives the death benefit, be it in the form of a lump sum or instalments. It ensures financial protection to your loved ones in your absence. Term insurance also makes sure your loved ones’ wealth is protected. In your absence, the family’s wealth and savings would have been depleted owing to the loss of income. Term insurance is important if you have loved ones dependent on your income or if your contribution to the family’s finances is significant.
If you survive the term policy, you do not receive any maturity benefits (except in return of premium plans wherein the premiums paid are returned on maturity) but by opting for a term insurance policy, you have ensured that your near and dear ones have a ring of financial protection around them.
Term insurance also comes with other benefits. It is cost-effective for people from different income brackets. It also gives you the option to increase coverage when you enter a new life stage, for example, marriage or the birth of a child. Then there is the added benefit of term insurance riders, which can be availed on payment of a nominal extra cost. These are add-on plans or features that you may opt, in case you need extra coverage for certain illnesses, disability, accidental death or waiver of premium. These benefits ensure your life goals are met and are financially protected in spite of the twists and turns there may be along the way.
You can make use of the term insurance calculator to calculate the insurance cover you need to help you meet the life goals of your family and to keep them secure financially.
There are tax benefits too with a term plan. There are different sections under which you can claim the tax benefits. But is term insurance covered under 80D?
Yes, term insurance is covered under 80D. You can claim the term insurance tax benefit under 80D if you add eligible riders to the policy. Let’s understand the details of the tax benefits of term insurance under 80D.
Tax Benefits of Section 80D in Term Insurance
The tax benefits under Section 80C and Section 10(10D), applicable to other life insurance plans, are also applicable to term plans. However, the term insurance tax benefit under 80D is different. Under Section 80D, you can claim an additional tax benefit on the premium paid for the specified riders added to your base term insurance plan.
In other words, if you add specified riders to your term insurance plan, the rider premiums paid would qualify for a tax deduction under Section 80D.
The limit for deduction is ₹25,000 for premiums paid for self, spouse, and children if you are below 60 years of age. If you are 60 years or older, the limit increases to ₹50,000.
Tax Benefits on Term Insurance Add-On Riders
The term insurance riders that allow tax deduction under Section 80D include the following -
Critical illness rider
Surgical care rider
Hospital cash allowance rider, etc.
Who Is Eligible to Claim Tax Deductions Under Section 80D?
Here is a list of eligibility criteria for claiming tax deductions under Section 80D -
Under Section 80D, both Individuals and HUFs are eligible for income tax deductions
The premium can be paid for self, spouse, children or dependent parents
Expenses incurred on medical insurance, preventive health check-ups, and medical expenses incurred for senior citizens can be claimed under this section
The deduction is available only under the old tax regime
What Is Section 80D of The Act & What It Normally Covers
Can benefits be claimed under 80D? The answer is yes, but under certain circumstances. Tax benefits are available under Section 80D for premiums in health insurance plans. However, insurers offer term insurance with critical illness riders or other health riders. If the term insurance policy offers a health rider and you have opted for the rider, then the premium on such a rider can be claimed under Section 80D. However, the premium amount of the term insurance would need to be claimed under Section 80C of the Act.
You can claim these benefits when you file your income tax returns, under the category of deduction under Section 80D, after clicking on the assessment year, only if you have opted for old tax regime. In addition, while claiming tax deductions when you have a health rider for your term insurance under Section 80D, here are some things you need to remember:
In a scenario where you have a critical illness term policy for yourself, spouse and children, and all of you are below 60, you are eligible for a tax deduction of up to Rs 25,000.
If you provided cover for your parents under a separate but similar policy and they are over 60, the deduction eligibility is up to Rs 50,000.
What Is Not Covered Under Section 80D?
Here are a few exclusions to Section 80D which you should know about -
Premiums paid in cash
If you don’t pay the premium, the 80D benefit will not be allowed
Employers’ contributions to group health insurance plans are not covered
Premiums paid for financially independent children would not be covered
How to Claim Tax Benefits Under Section 80D for Term Insurance
To claim tax benefits under Section 80D, here’s what you can do -
Add suitable health-related riders to your policy and assess the rider premium paid
Choose the old tax regime to claim the 80D deduction
When filing your returns, mention the rider premium paid and claim it under 80D deductions
Keep proofs of premium payments, which can be submitted when required
Term Insurance Comes Under Which Section 80C Or 80D
A term insurance policy allows deductions on the base premium paid under Section 80C. However, if you add eligible riders to your policy, the premium paid for such riders would qualify for an added deduction under Section 80D.
So, you can claim a tax benefit under both Section 80C and 80D if you buy a term insurance plan and add health-related riders to your policy. This would not only offer added tax benefits but also enhance the scope of coverage.
Key Takeaways
Term insurance plans can offer additional tax benefits under Section 80D of the Income Tax Act.
The term insurance tax benefit under Section 80D is available if you add eligible health-related riders to your term insurance policy.
Eligible riders for 80D deduction include the critical illness rider, surgical care rider, hospital benefit rider, etc.
You can claim a maximum deduction of up to ₹25,000 if you are below 60 and ₹50,000 if you are a senior citizen.
Premiums paid in cash, missed premiums, premiums for independent children, etc., are some instances when the 80D tax benefit is not allowed.
Conclusion
Term insurance is a pure protection plan that is aimed at securing the financial future of your loved ones and ensuring that the family’s life goals are met. An added benefit is that you can claim tax deductions on term insurance premiums, and your nominee/family can claim exemptions on the death payout under Section 80C and Section 10 (10D) of the Act, respectively. Further, Section 80D of the Act lets you claim tax benefits for certain riders you buy to cover medical expenses pertaining to critical illnesses or disabilities. Getting a term insurance policy can be a valuable gift you can give to your loved ones, so consider going for it.
FAQs
1. How to define the tenure of your term insurance?
The tenure of a term insurance plan is the period for which coverage is allowed against premature demise. Term insurance plans usually offer long-term coverage tenures for maximum protection. Some plans might also allow the whole life option, wherein you can enjoy coverage till 99 or 100 years of age.
You can choose a coverage tenure depending on your protection needs. It is better to opt for the longest possible tenure for optimum financial protection.
2. Can I have 2 term insurance policies?
You can have two or more term insurance policies for maximum coverage. There’s no restriction on the number of policies that you can have.
3. Is the term plan sum assured taxable?
The term plan sum assured paid on death is not taxable.
4. Is an income tax return (ITR) required for term insurance?
An income Tax Return (ITR) might be needed for term insurance if you opt for very high sum assured levels, where the premium is also higher. The insurance company needs the ITR to check your premium-paying capacity when choosing high coverage levels3.
5. How should I report Section 80D deductions in my tax return?
Mention the deduction under Chapter VI A when filing your returns and specify the amount to claim the tax benefit.
6. What are the deduction limits under Sections 80D and 80DD?
Section 80D allows a maximum deduction of up to ₹25,000 if you are below 60 and ₹50,000 if you are a senior citizen. You can claim an additional deduction of up to ₹25,000 if you pay premiums for your parents. If your parents are senior citizens, the limit increases to ₹50,000. So, under Section 80D, you can claim a maximum total deduction of up to ₹1 lakh u/s 8-D of the Income Tax Act.
Under Section 80DD, you can claim a maximum deduction of ₹75,000 if the disability is less than 80% or ₹1.25 lakhs if it exceeds 80%.
7. Is it possible to claim deductions under both 80C and 80D?
Yes, you can claim a tax deduction under 80C and 80D through eligible investments and expenses. Both sections are mutually exclusive and can help you lower your tax liability.
8. Can I claim 80D benefits for my parents without providing bills or receipts?
Usually, bills or receipts are required to claim 80D benefits. You can talk to a tax professional to understand the requirements of bills or receipts for claiming the benefit.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility
